data.title: Leverage Positions of Huang Licheng Show Significant Losses
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on January 10, monitoring by HyperInsight revealed that the address belonging to Huang Licheng, known as 'Maji Da Ge,' has seen its overall unrealized losses expand to $610,000. Previously, the address had achieved unrealized gains exceeding $1.4 million. The current holdings are as follows:

A 25x leveraged long position on 10,706 ETH, with a liquidation price of $3,000.16, is showing an unrealized loss of $460,000.

Additionally, a 10x leveraged long position on 86,000 HYPE, with a liquidation price of $12.12, is experiencing an unrealized loss of $150,000.

data.title: OKX Cuts Institutional Staff as Part of Global Restructuring
data.description:
Crypto exchange OKX has reduced headcount within its institutional business as part of a broader global restructuring, according to people familiar with the matter. The move reflects a recalibration of priorities as the company adjusts its operating footprint across regions.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/okx-cuts-institutional-staff-as-part-of-global-restructuring/

data.title: Polymarket Partners with Golden Globe Awards as Exclusive Prediction Market
data.description: According to PANews, decentralized prediction market platform Polymarket has announced its partnership as the exclusive prediction market collaborator for the Golden Globe Awards, as stated in an official announcement.

data.title: BNB Chain Foundation Acquires New Assets in $100 Million Incentive Program
data.description: According to PANews, BNB Chain announced that its foundation wallet has made new asset purchases as part of the $100 million incentive program. The acquisitions include $50,000 worth of $人生 and $50,000 worth of $踏马.

data.title: Inflation Data May Align with Economic Conditions by April, Says Fed Official
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve official Barkin has indicated that inflation data might not fully reflect the current economic situation until April.

data.title: CZ: US banks continue to increase their Bitcoin holdings during market downturns.
data.description: CZ retweeted a post on the X platform stating, "Wells Fargo just bought $383 million worth of Bitcoin; US banks are buying heavily," and commented, "While you're panic selling, US banks are continuously adding to their Bitcoin holdings."

data.title: Circle's USDC Circulation Sees Decline Over Seven Days
data.description: According to PANews, official data reveals that Circle issued approximately 5.4 billion USDC and redeemed around 5.7 billion USDC in the seven days leading up to January 8. This resulted in a decrease of about 300 million USDC in circulation. The total circulation of USDC stands at 74.9 billion, with reserves estimated at 75 billion USD. These reserves include approximately 50.7 billion USD in overnight reverse repurchase agreements, 13.7 billion USD in Treasury bills with maturities of less than three months, 9.9 billion USD in deposits at systemically important institutions, and around 700 million USD in other bank deposits.

data.title: Solana Spot ETF Experiences No Net Inflow or Outflow
data.description:
According to PANews, data from SoSoValue indicates that on January 9, Eastern Time, Solana spot ETFs experienced no net inflow or outflow. All eight Solana spot ETFs showed zero change throughout the day.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of Solana spot ETFs stands at $1.09 billion, with a Solana net asset ratio of 1.43%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $817 million.

data.title: Bitcoin OG Faces Significant Losses Amid Market Downturn
data.description:
According to Odaily, Onchain Lens monitoring reveals that a well-known Bitcoin OG is experiencing substantial floating losses exceeding $9 million on their long positions as the market continues to decline. Additionally, the cumulative loss in funding fees amounts to approximately $5.56 million.

Specifically, the Bitcoin OG's long positions in BTC and ETH are currently in a loss, while the SOL long position remains profitable.

data.title: Stablecoin Payment Cards Predicted to Be Key in Crypto by 2026
data.description: According to PANews, Dragonfly Managing Partner Haseeb Qureshi has stated that stablecoin-based payment cards are expected to become one of the most significant topics in the crypto sector by 2026. These cards aim to offer the benefits of blockchain technology while maintaining a payment experience familiar to consumers.

data.title: BingX joins Binance and Bitget in push toward multi-asset crypto trading
data.description:
BingX announced the launch of a new trading product tied to traditional assets. The new feature, called BingX TradFi, offers futures tied to traditional assets.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/bingx-new-trading-product-traditional-assets/

data.title: Grayscale Registers New ETFs Amidst Investor Interest in Delaware
data.description:
Grayscale registers new HYPE and BNB ETFs in Delaware, sparking investor intrigue. No SEC filings or market launches yet.
source: https://coincu.com/news/grayscale-registers-new-etfs/

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Discusses the Distinction Between 'Corposlop' and 'Sovereign Networks'
data.description:
According to PANews, Vitalik Buterin recently shared his thoughts on social media about the differences between 'corposlop web' and 'sovereign networks.' He defines 'corposlop' as a combination of corporate optimization, seemingly respectable branding, and unethical actions aimed at maximizing profit. Buterin describes 'corposlop' as soulless, trend-following, and a homogenized product that is both evil and awkward.

He notes that the concept of 'sovereignty' has evolved to mean protecting digital privacy through cryptographic technology and safeguarding one's thoughts from corporate mental warfare designed to exploit user attention and money. Buterin urges developers to create more sovereign tools, such as:

1. Privacy-protecting, locally prioritized applications.

2. Social media platforms where users control the content they see.

3. Financial tools that help users grow wealth without promoting high leverage or sports betting.

4. Open, privacy-focused, and locally friendly AI tools.

5. Applications, companies, and DAOs with a clear vision.

Buterin concludes by emphasizing the importance of maintaining sovereignty, rejecting 'corposlop,' and believing in something meaningful.

data.title: Galaxy's research director interprets the voting prospects of the cryptostructures bill: Passing it would be a major positive, while obstacles or suppression could dampen sentiment.
data.description: Galaxy Research Director Alex Thorn published an analysis on the X platform regarding the upcoming Senate Banking Committee vote on the Crypto Market Structure Bill on January 15th. He stated that the current Senate vote is 53 to 47, but a bill typically requires 60 votes to pass, meaning Republicans still need 7-10 Democratic senators to support the bill. Thorn added that the Crypto Market Structure Bill is significant, addressing issues such as the classification of DeFi under anti-money laundering rules, the handling of stablecoin reserve yields, protection of non-custodial developers, and the SEC's power to authorize or restrict token issuance. If passed, it would be a major bullish catalyst for cryptocurrency adoption. If it fails to pass, while the overall impact on the crypto industry's fundamentals would be relatively small, it could lead to negative market sentiment.

data.title: Bitcoin Top Is Not In At $126,000, According To The Business Cycle, Here’s Why
data.description:
Crypto expert Plan C has alluded to the business cycle to explain why the Bitcoin top isn’t in despite the flagship crypto’s run to $126,000 last year. This comes as BTC struggles to hold above the psychological $90,000 level, having lost most of its gains from the start of the year. Why The Bitcoin Top Isn’t In Yet Based On The Business Cycle In an X post, Plan C suggested that it doesn’t make sense to call the Bitcoin top when the business cycle hasn’t even crossed 50. The expert noted that BTC bull market peaks have historically occurred when the business cycle reaches between 55 and 65
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin/bitcoin-top-not-in-at-126000/

data.title: Vitalik: "Corposlop" is a soulless, evil, and clumsy homogenization.
data.description: Vitalik Buterin analyzed the difference between the "corposlop web" (corporate spam web) and the "sovereign web" in a social media post. He defined "corposlop" as a combination of corporate optimization capabilities, a seemingly respectable brand image, and dishonest behavior in pursuit of profit maximization. Buterin described "corposlop" as a soulless, trend-following, and both evil and clumsy homogenization. He pointed out that the meaning of "sovereignty" has expanded to include protecting digital privacy through encryption and protecting one's mind from the psychic warfare of corporations aimed at extracting user attention and money. Buterin called on developers to build more sovereign tools, such as: 1. Privacy-preserving, native-first applications. 2. Social media platforms where users can control what they see. 3. Financial tools that help users grow wealth rather than encouraging 50x leverage or sports betting. 4. Open, privacy-focused, and native-friendly AI tools. 5. Applications, companies, and DAOs with a clear vision. Vitalik concluded by saying that sovereignty should be maintained, corposlop should be rejected, and there should be belief in something. He summarized it as: "Maintain sovereignty, reject corposlop, and believe in something."

data.title: Spot Bitcoin ETFs lose $681M in first week of 2026 as risk appetite fades
data.description:
Spot Bitcoin ETFs reversed early inflows in 2026 and posted four straight days of outflows as fading rate-cut hopes and rising geopolitical risks pushed investors into risk-off positioning.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/spot-bitcoin-etfs-lose-681m-first-week-2026-risk-off?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound
